rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee/eq/const in component type annexes for interface behavior; use implementation annexes only for assign clauses tied to subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible feature names or explicitly declared constants/variables in AGREE expressions; do not reference AADL properties as if they are AGREE variables.
generic_rules: Contracts should express interface invariants using named ports or constants, not internal implementation details.
Boolean logic in guarantees should derive from observable system behavior, not internal state.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Constants and equations must be declared before being referenced in assume/guarantee clauses.
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) directly into AGREE expressions without explicit port or constant mapping.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless mediated through owned outputs or variables.
